data.title: THE OPERATING LOOP.
blocks: · Name the work. What recurring task are you building a workflow for? Be specific. "Writing" is not a workstream. "First-d
· Choose the surface. Claude AI (chat) for thinking, planning, drafting, and explanation. Claude Code for codebase and scr
Prepare context. What does Claude need to know to do this task well? What source files, prior outputs, constraints, or a sensitive data, unpublished material, confidential names?
· Specify the output. What artifact should Claude produce? What format, length, tone, and verification standard apply?
· Let Claude propose a plan. For multi-step tasks, ask Claude to summarize its plan before it acts. This is an approval ga

data.title: THE SCOPE STACK.
blocks: Account instructions — Stable personal preferences your usual role, tone defaults, citation style, privacy basics
Project instructions — Stable project rules audience, output format, approved sources, access limits, verification standard
Task prompt — Current job the specific ask, context for this instance, any one-time constraints
Human gate — Your verification action what you check before the output leaves your desk

data.title: PROJECTS: THE ORGANIZING UNIT
blocks: · You run this kind of work repeatedly
· The work benefits from persistent context (background, style, source list, constraints)
· The output has a verification path
· A brief description of the workstream and its purpose
· Stable instructions (what Claude should do, what it should not do, what format to use)

data.title: MEMORY: USEFUL AND RISKY
blocks: Claude's memory features allow context to persist across conversations. This can reduce friction for recurring users: yo
Memory: Useful and Risky — Memory is useful. It is also a privacy surface and a staleness risk.

data.title: TASK CLASSIFICATION: THE WORKFLOW FOUNDATION
blocks: · What does Claude produce? (Text, code, file, plan, analysis, something else)
· Can I verify this output without Claude's help? (Yes, partially, no)
· What is the risk if this output is wrong? (Low, medium, high)
· What happens if a sensitive piece of information ends up in the output by mistake?
Claude AI (chat) Thinking, drafting, explanation, critique, brainstorming. You need fast conversation with human review before anything l

data.title: THE PERSONAL WORKFLOW CANVAS.
blocks: Workstream name — What is this workflow for?
Claude surface — Chat, Code, Cowork, or human-only?
Allowed inputs — What context and files can go in?
Forbidden inputs — What must never be included?

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Treating a master prompt as a workflow.
Using memory as a substitute for context design.
Loading project instructions with every possible rule.
Optimizing the workflow before diagnosing the task.
Setting a workflow and forgetting it.

data.title: BRIDGE TO CHAPTER 10.
blocks: This chapter gave you the design template. Chapter 10 asks you to use it.
